name: Oatmeal Yeast Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften yeast in lukewarm water. (Use warm water for dry yeast.)
2. Pour scalded milk over sugar, salt, and butter. Cool to lukewarm.
3. Stir in egg and 1 cup flour. Add softened yeast and oatmeal. Mix until a soft dough forms.
4. Turn out onto lightly floured board or canvas; knead until smooth and satiny, about 10 minutes.
5. Form dough into a ball and place in greased bowl. Brush lightly with melted shortening. Cover and let rise in warm place until double in size, about 1 hour.
6. Punch dough down; cover; let rest 10 minutes.
7. Shape dough to form crescents, spirals, cloverleaf, and Parker House rolls.
8. Place cloverleaf rolls in greased muffin cups; place other shapes on greased cookie sheets.
9. Brush with melted shortening. Cover; let rise in warm place until nearly double in size, about 45 minutes.
10. Bake in preheated moderate oven (375°F) 15 to 18 minutes or until golden brown.
Note: Cook Quaker Oats (quick or old fashioned, uncooked) according to package directions.
